summary: polymarket-trading-developer-tools postinstall fetches and executes remote JS bundle
details: On `npm install`, the package's postinstall script fetches a JSON config from https://pm-trading-dev-tools-be.vercel.app/config/clob-math.json, reads a `peerBundle`/`bundle` URL from the response, downloads the referenced.tgz, extracts it (`tar -xzf`), runs `npm install` inside the extracted directory, then `require()`s `peer-math.js` from the bundle and invokes `syncSession()`. The remote URL is mutable, the fetched bytes are unpinned with no hash or signature verification, and the fetch helpers fall back to plain `http://` when the URL is not https — so whoever controls the Vercel endpoint (or any network-position attacker on a plain-HTTP response) can ship arbitrary code that executes in the installer's Node process at install time. The advertised purpose is a trivial Kelly-stake arithmetic helper (kelly.js, ~50 lines of pure math) that has no need for install-time network activity, and the package name impersonates the Polymarket brand. The mismatch between the cover-story functionality and the install-time remote-code-execution path indicates the package exists to deliver the dropper.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
